eigentum.message: Civilian ownership and fee simple absolute converge strongly as each system’s broad default ownership form.
eigentum.divergence: The fit is not perfect because civilian ownership remains anchored in a codified menu of rights rather than common-law estates and equity.

erbbaurecht.message: The long, inheritable building right often behaves like an institutionalized long lease from a common-law viewpoint.
erbbaurecht.divergence: Civil law treats it as a named real right in its own standing, whereas common law usually channels the pattern through leasehold and covenant structure.

niessbrauch.message: Usufruct-like enjoyment maps most closely to life-estate logic when the focus is present use and fruits rather than title.
niessbrauch.divergence: Common law spreads the same economic profile across life estates, trusts, and contractual occupation devices rather than a single unitary usufruct.

grunddienstbarkeit.message: Servitude and easement doctrine line up closely as durable, non-possessory rights over another parcel.
grunddienstbarkeit.divergence: Residual divergence comes from doctrinal packaging and registration architecture rather than the functional bundle itself.

hypothek.message: Both systems recognize a durable security right aimed at collateral value rather than ordinary occupation.
hypothek.divergence: The civilian mortgage stays more explicitly accessory and codified, while common law layers legal and equitable foreclosure traditions over the same terrain.

pfandrecht.message: There is some convergence at the level of secured credit, but the fit to common-law mortgage categories is loose.
pfandrecht.divergence: Possession-sensitive pledge structure and accessory logic do not translate cleanly into the standard common-law land-security template.

dian_quan.message: No stable common-law estate captures the redemption-centered structure of dian quan without distortion.
dian_quan.divergence: The right combines possession, use, time, and redemption logic in a way that falls between leasehold, security, and limited ownership categories.

juzhuquan.message: A habitation right partially resembles a protected occupation licence in common-law terms.
juzhuquan.divergence: Civil law can package residence as a named real right, while common law typically disperses the same social function across weaker, more situational devices.

tudi_chengbao_jingyingquan.message: No common-law estate captures the collective-to-household land contracting structure of PRC rural property.
tudi_chengbao_jingyingquan.divergence: The right is embedded in PRC's collective land ownership system, which has no structural parallel in common-law jurisdictions. Agricultural tenancy and leasehold capture fragments of the economic function but miss the collective allocation mechanism entirely.

zhaijidi_shiyongquan.message: No common-law estate captures the one-household-one-plot allocation model of PRC rural residential land.
zhaijidi_shiyongquan.divergence: The right arises from administrative allocation by the village collective, not market transaction. Common-law systems have no equivalent mechanism for distributing residential land use rights within a collective ownership framework.

liuzhiquan.message: Common-law possessory liens share the retention-until-payment logic, but lack the in rem security interest structure of the civil-law form.
liuzhiquan.divergence: Civil law treats lien as a named property right with priority rules and enforcement procedures; common law tends to treat possessory liens as equitable or statutory remedies without a unified real-right classification.

nongyuquan.message: No common-law estate cleanly maps to a codified, term-limited agricultural use right with numerus clausus status.
nongyuquan.divergence: Common law channels agricultural use through leasehold, profit à prendre, or contractual arrangements rather than a dedicated real right. The 20-year statutory ceiling and the abolition of the predecessor 永佃權 reflect policy choices with no common-law parallel.

qufen_dishangquan.message: Common-law strata title and airspace rights serve partially analogous functions, but lack the codified numerus clausus packaging.
qufen_dishangquan.divergence: The civil-law form is a named real right over a defined stratum; common law achieves similar outcomes through a mix of strata title legislation, airspace conveyances, and easements, none of which map one-to-one to the sectional superficies concept.
